Politics.headline: Iran rejects ceasefire deal as Trump's deadline for attacks on infrastructure nears
Politics.detail: Iran rejected a 45-day ceasefire proposal and said it wants a permanent end to the war as U.S. President Donald Trump's ultimatum to make a deal ticked closer with an expanded threat of strikes against the Islamic Republic to include all power plants and bridges. French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot said attacks targeting civilian and energy infrastructure “are barred by the rules of war, international law” and would surely trigger reprisals from Iran.

Politics.headline: Trump seizes on rescue of downed airman to recast unpopular Iran war
Politics.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump was on the verge of a crisis in the Iran war, faced with the rare instance of an American airman shot down and stranded deep inside enemy territory, but the airman's daring weekend rescue gave the U.S. president the chance to quickly flip the script. Standing before cameras on Monday, Trump recast the perilous operation as a providential military triumph, leaning in to its cinematic elements ​to project strength and command of a five-week-old war that remains deeply unpopular with U.S. voters.

Politics.headline: Vance visits Hungary to shore up Orban's support before Sunday vote
Politics.detail: U.S. Vice President JD Vance arrived in Hungary on Tuesday in a bid to turn the tide of an election campaign where long-serving Prime Minister Viktor Orbán, a close ally of President Donald Trump, is trailing in the polls. Vance's two-day trip, where he is scheduled to hold an official visit with Orbán and later appear at one of his campaign rallies, offered the clearest sign yet that the Trump administration is going all-in for an Orbán victory when Hungarians go to the polls on Sunday.

Politics.headline: US Supreme Court clears path for Steve Bannon criminal case dismissal
Politics.detail: The Supreme Court has cleared the path for a potential dismissal of Steve Bannon's criminal case. The move frees a trial judge to act on the Republican administration's pending request to dismiss Bannon's conviction and indictment "in the interests of justice."

Politics.headline: China expresses concern over Iran conflict and calls for ceasefire
Politics.detail: China's Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Mao Ning stated that the ongoing conflict has caused heavy casualties and damage, hitting the security and stability of regional countries and impacting the world economy and energy security. China is deeply concerned and believes that prolonging or escalating the conflict does not serve any party's interest, urging all sides to play a constructive role in de-escalating the situation and facilitating peace talks.
Politics.source: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the People's Republic of China

Finance & Economy.headline: Georgia: Staff Concluding Statement of the 2026 Article IV Mission
Finance & Economy.detail: The IMF released a statement regarding its staff's conclusions after a recent mission to Georgia, focusing on the country's economic policies and financial stability.

Finance & Economy.headline: Macroeconomic Impact of Accelerating Public Infrastructure Investments in Morocco
Finance & Economy.detail: The IMF published a report analyzing the potential macroeconomic effects of increasing public infrastructure investments in Morocco.

Finance & Economy.headline: Public Spending Efficiency in the Health and Education Sectors in Morocco
Finance & Economy.detail: A new report by the IMF examines the efficiency of public spending within the health and education sectors in Morocco.

Finance & Economy.headline: Event | Global Financial Stability Report
Finance & Economy.detail: The IMF is hosting a panel discussion on capital flows to emerging markets, specifically focusing on the role of global nonbank investors, as part of the April 2026 Global Financial Stability Report.

Finance & Economy.headline: Vietnam aims to shore up battered stocks with stabilisation fund, documents show
Finance & Economy.detail: Vietnam is considering establishing a stabilization fund and other measures, including corporate share buyback incentives and limits on daily trading bands, to support its struggling stock market.

Technology.headline: AI slop got better, so now maintainers have more work
Technology.detail: Once AI bug reports become plausible, someone still has to verify them.

Technology.headline: AMD's AI director slams Claude Code for becoming dumber and lazier since last update
Technology.detail: 'Claude cannot be trusted to perform complex engineering tasks' according to GitHub ticket.

Technology.headline: Anthropic closes door on subscription use of OpenClaw
Technology.detail: The company is having trouble meeting user demand.

Technology.headline: 'Moon joy!' Artemis 2's crew sets a distance record, documents lunar far side and heads back toward Earth
Technology.detail: The Artemis 2 crew has set a distance record while documenting the lunar far side and is now heading back towards Earth.

Technology.headline: Data visualization all-stars unveil Ridge AI with $2.6M to fix the analytics problem for SaaS apps
Technology.detail: Data visualization veterans Ellie Fields and Jeffrey Heer are launching Ridge AI from stealth with $2.6M from Madrona and high-profile angels, aiming to help SaaS companies present interactive analytics to their customers.

Science.headline: Scientists solve 30-year mystery of a hidden nutrient that protects the brain and fights cancer
Science.detail: Scientists have uncovered how our bodies absorb queuosine, a rare micronutrient crucial for brain health, memory, stress response, and cancer defense, by identifying the gene SLC35F2 as the gateway into cells. This breakthrough opens new possibilities for therapies and highlights how diet and gut microbes profoundly shape human health.

Science.headline: Your Vitamin D Levels in Midlife Could Shape Your Brain Decades Later
Science.detail: Vitamin D levels in midlife may play a bigger role in long-term brain health than previously thought; a study following nearly 800 people over 16 years found that those with higher vitamin D levels in their 30s and 40s had lower levels of tau protein later in life, which is linked to Alzheimer's disease.

Science.headline: Scientists Say 7 Days of Meditation Can Rewire Your Brain
Science.detail: A single week of intensive meditation and mind-body practices led to measurable changes across the brain and body, including improved brain efficiency, boosted immune signaling, and increased natural pain relief chemicals. The effects even promoted neuron growth and stronger brain connectivity, mirroring psychedelic-like brain states without any drugs.

Science.headline: Water on the moon? New study narrows down the mostly likely locations
Science.detail: A new study suggests water likely accumulated on the moon slowly over billions of years, rather than during one big event, and identifies the moon's oldest and darkest craters as the most likely locations for ice. Future lunar explorers could potentially mine this ice for drinking water or rocket fuel.

Science.headline: Artemis II astronauts swung by the moon, broke an Apollo record, and saw an eclipse
Science.detail: The Artemis II mission, a test flight of the Orion spacecraft, involved the crew taking geological observations of around 35 places of interest on the lunar surface and snapping thousands of photos, achieving a never-before-seen view of the far side of the moon. As the Orion capsule looped around the back side of the moon, the crew broke the Apollo 13 record for distance from Earth.

Health.headline: World Health Day 2026: Together for health. Stand with science
Health.detail: World Health Day 2026, observed on April 7, promotes scientific collaboration to protect the health of people, animals, plants, and the planet, emphasizing the 'One Health' approach and urging the public to engage with evidence and support science-led solutions. The campaign is anchored by the International One Health Summit and the Global Forum of WHO Collaborating Centres.

Health.headline: Drastic UK Aid Cuts Hit Fragile African Health Systems
Health.detail: Sweeping UK aid cuts have drastically reduced direct bilateral funding to African countries, posing a severe threat to the continent's most fragile health systems, with officials confirming a steep 31% multi-year reduction of the foreign aid budget. The cuts are motivated by a ramp up in national defense spending.

Health.headline: World Health Day marked through various activities across China
Health.detail: World Health Day is being observed across China with activities promoting health awareness, including eye exercises for children, recognition of traditional Chinese medicinal herbs, and health examinations. The day marks the anniversary of the WHO's founding and raises awareness of pressing global health issues.

Health.headline: Leading with Science and Evidence in a Time of Retreat
Health.detail: As World Health Day emphasizes leading with science, the WHO is prioritizing a One Health approach, but global health security commitments are being dismantled at an alarming pace, including the rescinding of over $500 million in federal contracts for mRNA vaccine research by the United States. These cuts weaken capacities to protect against biological threats.

Health.headline: Resident doctors' strike has torpedoed pay rises and training posts, says Wes Streeting
Health.detail: Resident doctors in England are facing a halt on new training posts due to the ongoing strike. The strike has had a negative impact on pay rises.

Sports.headline: Richmond to offer Zak Butters mega contract, could be biggest deal in AFL history
Sports.detail: Richmond is reportedly willing to offer Port Adelaide free agent Zak Butters a record-breaking contract as they look to rebuild their team. The Western Bulldogs and Geelong are also interested, but Richmond has the salary cap space to potentially outbid them.

Sports.headline: Blue Jays RHP Max Scherzer Leaves Start Against Dodgers After Second Inning
Sports.detail: Max Scherzer, the Blue Jays' right-handed pitcher, had to leave his start against the Dodgers after pitching only two innings due to forearm tendinitis. This is a developing story, and further updates are expected.

Sports.headline: Australia Legend David Warner Charged With Drunk Driving, Arrested
Sports.detail: Australian cricket legend David Warner has been charged with drunk driving and has been arrested. The details surrounding the incident are still emerging, but the news has sent shockwaves through the cricketing world.

Sports.headline: Wembanyama suffers left rib contusion vs 76ers, his status for last 3 regular-season games unknown
Sports.detail: Victor Wembanyama sustained a left rib contusion during the game against the 76ers, casting doubt on his availability for the remaining three regular-season games. The severity of the injury and the timeline for his return are currently unknown.

Sports.headline: Jokic's 35 points, 13 rebounds, 13 assists help the Nuggets get past the Trail Blazers 137-132 in OT
Sports.detail: Nikola Jokic delivered a dominant performance with 35 points, 13 rebounds, and 13 assists, leading the Denver Nuggets to a 137-132 overtime victory against the Trail Blazers. Jokic's all-around brilliance was instrumental in securing the win for the Nuggets in a closely contested match.

Entertainment.headline: The Devil Wears Prada 2 trailer: Anne Hathaway's Andy is roped in to save Meryl Streep's Miranda from a 'scandal', but can't escape her jibes
Entertainment.detail: A trailer has been released for The Devil Wears Prada 2, showing Anne Hathaway's character returning to help Meryl Streep's Miranda, but still facing her sharp remarks.

Entertainment.headline: Offset Shot in Florida, spokesperson says he is stable
Entertainment.detail: Rapper Offset was reportedly shot near a casino in Hollywood, Florida, but his representative has confirmed that he is in stable condition.

Entertainment.headline: Angelina Jolie visit sparks revival of historic Battambang theaters in Cambodia
Entertainment.detail: Angelina Jolie's visit to Battambang, Cambodia, has inspired renewed interest and efforts to revitalize the city's historic theaters.

Entertainment.headline: Jessica Sanchez to share stage with OPM icons Regine, Gary, Martin at PH concert
Entertainment.detail: Jessica Sanchez will be performing alongside Filipino music icons Regine Velasquez, Gary Valenciano, and Martin Nievera in an upcoming concert in the Philippines.

Entertainment.headline: Bianca Gonzalez appeals to media outlets to stop showing prices of luxury items
Entertainment.detail: Filipina television host Bianca Gonzalez has requested media outlets to refrain from displaying the prices of luxury goods in their reports.

Crime & Justice.headline: Mistrial after report that girl killed in 1986 was placed in used body bag
Crime & Justice.detail: A new allegation that an 11-year-old Connecticut girl, who was raped and killed in 1986, was placed in a used body bag led to a mistrial in a murder case due to concerns about evidence contamination.

Crime & Justice.headline: Tuesday briefing: What is driving the record rise in stalking offences?
Crime & Justice.detail: Disturbing data reveals a surge in stalking offences recorded by police in England and Wales over the past decade, with cases charged by the Crown Prosecution Service also reaching a record high, leaving victims in constant fear.

Crime & Justice.headline: Father of Charlie Cosser calls for knife crime lessons at school for nine-year-olds
Crime & Justice.detail: The father of Charlie Cosser, a teenager who was fatally stabbed at a party, is advocating for knife crime education in schools starting at age nine to address the issue proactively.

Crime & Justice.headline: Arrest of former Australian SAS soldier over alleged war crimes is a critical step toward global justice and accountability efforts
Crime & Justice.detail: The Australian Federal Police arrested Ben Roberts-Smith, a former special forces soldier, for alleged war crimes in Afghanistan between 2006 and 2012, marking a significant move towards justice and accountability following the Brereton Inquiry.

Crime & Justice.headline: Syria: a Commission for the Missing “still at the experimental stage”
Crime & Justice.detail: Syria's National Commission for the Forcibly Disappeared, created in May 2025, faces challenges in maintaining public trust and governmental support almost a year later.

Education.headline: Meghalaya SSLC Toppers 2026: Check List Of Students Who Scored 100% Marks
Education.detail: The Meghalaya Board of School Education (MBOSE) has announced the SSLC (Class 10) results, including a list of students who achieved perfect scores. The results highlight the academic achievements of students in the state.

Education.headline: ICAI Reschedules CA Intermediate Exams; CA Final To Be Held Twice A Year from May 2026
Education.detail: The Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) has rescheduled the CA Intermediate exams and announced that the CA Final exams will be held twice a year starting May 2026. This change aims to provide more flexibility for students pursuing chartered accountancy.

Education.headline: Graduate Pharmacy Aptitude Test Result 2026 Out, Here's Direct Link To Check
Education.detail: The results for the Graduate Pharmacy Aptitude Test (GPAT) 2026 have been released, and a direct link is provided for candidates to check their scores. GPAT is a national-level entrance exam for admission to postgraduate pharmacy programs.

Education.headline: BukSU College of Education clinches Top 6 spot at the 1st National Edukalidad Competitions
Education.detail: Bukidnon State University's (BukSU) College of Education secured a Top 6 position at the 1st National Edukalidad Competitions, which showcased the skills and innovation of future educators. The event featured competitions in research, teaching, and instructional materials design.

Education.headline: FSU graduate programs achieve top marks in 2026 U.S. News & World Report rankings
Education.detail: Florida State University's graduate programs have been recognized for excellence in the 2026 U.S. News & World Report rankings, with 16 programs ranking in the Top 25 among public universities and six achieving the No. 1 spot in Florida. The rankings reflect FSU's strength in various academic fields, including healthcare, law, and the arts.

Lifestyle.headline: 9 clear signs from the universe that your soulmate has arrived
Lifestyle.detail: A spiritual guide lists signs that the universe is sending to indicate your soulmate has arrived, such as synchronicities and a feeling of deep connection.

Lifestyle.headline: 5 Healthy Air Fryer Chicken Snacks for Weight Loss: High-Protein and Low-Calorie
Lifestyle.detail: This article provides recipes for air fryer chicken snacks that are high in protein and low in calories, making them suitable for weight loss.

Lifestyle.headline: 3 most important factors that cause skin barrier damage: Tips for prevention
Lifestyle.detail: This article discusses the main causes of skin barrier damage and offers tips on how to prevent it, helping readers maintain healthy skin.

Lifestyle.headline: Wawa recalls 4 drink products over undeclared allergen
Lifestyle.detail: Wawa has issued a recall for four of its drink products due to an undeclared allergen, advising customers with allergies to avoid these items.

Lifestyle.headline: Blink and you'll miss Kate Middleton's teeny-tiny $450 handbag at the Royal Family's Easter celebration
Lifestyle.detail: Kate Middleton was seen with a small $450 handbag at the Royal Family's Easter celebration.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Rise Before The Fall: In Full Bloom before winter
Opinion/Editorial.detail: This opinion piece discusses observations and reflections on life and the world, written on April 07, 2026.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Quality education remains elusive
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial discusses the lack of breakthrough in lifting Filipino students out of their dismal proficiency levels despite the large budget allocated to education.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: When I get abused just for dancing, it shows how far hatred of politicians has gone
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Stella Creasy discusses the abuse she received for dancing and how it reflects the extent of hatred towards politicians.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Trump's chaotic war on Iran has dragged into its sixth week because he is fighting an adversary he doesn't understand
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Nesrine Malik analyzes Trump's war on Iran, highlighting his misunderstanding of the adversary and the resulting chaos.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Maine's race to the top on taxes
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Janet Mills, after opposing similar tax hikes, is now supporting a millionaires tax for Maine while running against Graham Platner in the Senate primary.

World.headline: Three killed in shooting near Israeli consulate in Turkiye's Istanbul
World.detail: A shootout outside the Israeli Consulate in Istanbul, Turkey, resulted in the death of one gunman and injuries to two others, according to local officials.

World.headline: Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make deal or U.S. will unleash 'hell'
World.detail: Former President Trump has issued a warning to Iran, stating they have 48 hours to reach a deal or face severe consequences from the United States.

World.headline: Australia charges ex-soldier Roberts-Smith with Afghan war crimes
World.detail: Former Australian soldier Ben Roberts-Smith has been charged with five war crime murders allegedly committed in Afghanistan.

World.headline: Ukraine, Russia kill three civilians each in tit-for-tat drone attacks
World.detail: Ukraine and Russia have exchanged deadly drone attacks, resulting in the deaths of three civilians on each side.

World.headline: One killed and buildings damaged as magnitude 7.4 earthquake strikes Indonesia
World.detail: A magnitude 7.4 earthquake struck Indonesia, causing damage to buildings and resulting in at least one fatality.

Zimbabwe.headline: Business welcomes new series of ZiG notes
Zimbabwe.detail: The business community has expressed optimism following the introduction of the new series of ZiG notes, anticipating that it will ease transactional challenges.

Zimbabwe.headline: Unifreight eyes 100m kg tobacco volume in 2026
Zimbabwe.detail: Logistics company Unifreight is aiming to handle 100 million kg of tobacco in 2026, leveraging the ongoing tobacco marketing season.

Zimbabwe.headline: Vic Falls allure spawns tourist rush
Zimbabwe.detail: Victoria Falls is experiencing a surge in tourist arrivals, boosting the local tourism sector.

Zimbabwe.headline: Zim breaks new ground . . . Harvests tobacco in non-traditional growing areas
Zimbabwe.detail: Zimbabwe is achieving success in tobacco farming in regions previously not known for tobacco cultivation.

Zimbabwe.headline: New ZiG notes start circulating, exchange rate spikes
Zimbabwe.detail: Following the introduction of new ZiG banknotes, the exchange rate has experienced a notable increase.
